data.title: Why Are Stock Market Futures Up Today, 2/12/26? Inflation Report Ahead
data.description:
U.S. stock futures traded higher late Thursday night after a weak day on Wall Street, while traders waited for a key inflation report due Friday morning. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were up 0.23%, 0.09%, and 0.16%, respectively, at 9:48 p.m. EDT on February 12.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In Thursday's session, the major indexes fell as worries about artificial-intelligence disruption spread to sectors such as real estate, trucking, and software. The S&P 500 dropped about 1.6%, the Nasdaq Composite fell roughly 2%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average lost nearly 670 points, or 1.3%.

In after-hours trading, Applied Materials (AMAT) jumped 11% after strong earnings and guidance. Airbnb (ABNB) gained around 2% on upbeat outlook comments, while Pinterest (PINS) slid 14% following weak quarterly results and a soft forecast.

All of the "Magnificent Seven" technology stocks finished lower. Cisco Systems (CSCO) fell 12% after disappointing guidance, and Apple (AAPL) dropped 5%, its biggest one-day decline since April 2025.

Investors are now focused on Friday's consumer price index report. Economists expect inflation to rise 2.5% from a year ago and 0.3% from the prior month.

data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Nova Lake Die Sizes Leak Sends Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Sliding
data.description:
Some interesting news slipped out recently about chip stock Intel (INTC), specifically about the Nova Lake processor lineup. Intel has a lot riding on the Nova Lake processor, which makes the fact that a leak emerged about these chips all the more unsettling. The news that slipped out focused on die sizes, and gave an advance look at the Nova Lake beyond what we have heard so far. Investors were not pleased, though, as evidenced by a nearly 3% loss in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The new word suggests that the Nova Lake line will have eight Coyote Cove P-cores along with 32 Arctic Wolf E-cores. That means around 110 square millimeters, which is quite a step from Nova Lake's predecessor. That processor measured about 150 square millimeters. That, coupled with the fact that the Nova Lake will be made using TSMC's (TSM) N2 process, means that the Nova Lake line will likely not be inexpensive to make.

It will help that most laptop Nova Lake processors will be made in-house at Intel's Arizona fab. And given that laptop processors outsell desktop processors by roughly seven to three these days, the costs may not be all that egregious after all. Still, knowing what we know so far suggests that higher prices than expected may show up later.

Wedbush Likes It

New reports suggest a reason to like Intel beyond that, though, as Intel is looking to put a $100 million commitment into SambaNova Systems. This might not sound like a great idea on the surface, given how aggressively Intel has been pinching pennies of late. But Wedbush analysts suggest that this could be the start of something truly impressive.

Reports note that Wedbush is calling the move a "…meaningful step in reshaping Intel's role in high-end compute." Given that a lot of computing power is moving to artificial intelligence, and SambaNova has something of a presence therein, Intel's connection to SambaNova could fuel substantial advancement in the higher-end computing sector. The fact that Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan has a personal connection to SambaNova, however, may taint the deal a bit.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on eight Buys, 20 Holds and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 100.12% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $48.32 per share implies 3.38% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Why Are Memory Chip Stocks Rising Today? SanDisk (SNDK) Leads the Gain
data.description:
Memory Chip Stocks, SanDisk (SNDK), Micron (MU), Western Digital (WDC), and Seagate Technology (STX), all closed higher on Thursday after Japan-based memory maker Kioxia gave strong guidance and pointed to continued demand from AI data centers.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Kioxia, a producer of NAND flash storage chips, forecast full-year sales and operating income above analyst expectations. The company also said some customers are already trying to secure supply for 2027 and 2028, even though memory contracts are normally signed about one year in advance.

The company's finance chief added that tight supply is leading to a sharp rise in selling prices.

What the News Signals for Memory Prices

Memory companies' earnings depend heavily on chip prices. Over the past year, profits improved because supply was tight and prices stayed high. Kioxia's comments now suggest that shortage may last longer than expected.

In addition, NAND flash is widely used in solid-state drives inside cloud servers. As companies continue building AI infrastructure, they need large storage capacity to hold training data and outputs. Because customers are securing supply years in advance, it suggests they expect demand to remain strong.

What Pushed These Memory Stocks Higher

SanDisk moved the most, closing more than 5% higher, because it manufactures NAND chips with Kioxia through a joint venture and is directly exposed to pricing. Micron also gained after recently saying shipments of its next-generation high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips began earlier than planned.

Western Digital and Seagate, which sell storage products used in data centers, often move with expectations for memory pricing.

Memory stocks had cooled earlier this year after a strong rally, but the latest update reassured investors that elevated chip prices, which support profits, may continue for now.

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Tumble on AI Disruption Fears ahead of Key Inflation Data
data.description:
The S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed Thursday deep in negative territory as tech stocks extended their losses.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Fears of AI disruption and overspending are in the spotlight again. Cisco (CSCO) closed with a 12% loss after its earnings report was overshadowed by a weaker-than-expected adjusted gross margin. In addition, Apple (AAPL), International Business Machines (IBM), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and Palantir (PLTR) all finished with drawdowns of at least 3%.

Concerning signals from the labor market have also unnerved investors. For the week ended February 7, initial jobless claims fell by 5,000 to 227,000, higher than the consensus estimate of 223,000. Continuing jobless claims increased by 21,000 to 1.862 million and were also above the expectation of 1.85 million. Initial jobless claims serve as a gauge of layoffs, while continuing jobless claims track the number of people receiving unemployment benefits and reflect ongoing unemployment.

On Wednesday, the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) announced that nonfarm payrolls increased by 130,000 in January, double the estimate of 65,000. At the same time, the BLS also revised total nonfarm payroll additions in 2025 to 181,000 from 584,000, marking the worst year for hiring outside of a recession since 2003.

Elsewhere, the U.S. has suspended a number of proposed restrictive measures against Chinese tech companies, potentially as a move to ease trade tensions following the completion of a trade truce between the two sides last October. These include sales of TP-Link routers, Chinese electric trucks and buses, and the U.S. operations of China Unicom and China Mobile, according to Reuters.

On Friday morning, the BLS will release January's Consumer Price Index (CPI), providing the market with a key inflation gauge. Economists expect prices to rise by 2.5% year-over-year, down from 2.7% in December. The data will likely affect the odds of a 25 bps rate cut at the March 18 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting, which have fallen to 7.8% from 23.2% over the past week, according to the CME FedWatch tool. A higher-than-expected reading could send the odds even lower, as higher rates work against inflationary pressures by increasing borrowing costs.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 1.57% loss, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) fell by 2.04%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Why TD Cowen Thinks D-Wave (QBTS) Isn't Just Another Quantum Story
data.description:
Top TD Cowen analyst Krish Sankar initiated coverage on D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) with a Buy rating. The 5-star analyst said D-Wave already earns real revenue, which sets it apart from many quantum companies still in the research stage. He said continued demand for the company's Advantage system sales and cloud usage should support growth in the coming years.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Why Sankar Is Bullish on QBTS Stock

Sankar said D-Wave's technology is already being used for practical tasks like scheduling and logistics. As more companies test quantum tools, he expects adoption to expand.

TD Cowen projects revenue of about $26 million for CY25, rising to roughly $135 million by 2028, implying more than 70% annual growth. Enterprise systems can also sell for $20 million to $40 million per unit with high margins.

Sankar also pointed to D-Wave's recent acquisition of Quantum Circuit Inc. technology, which could speed development of future processors and open the door to AI and machine-learning workloads later this decade.

Sankar ranks #10 out of 12,108 analysts tracked on TipRanks. He has a 66% success rate and an impressive average return per rating of 41.90%.

What Could Drive the Stock

The analyst said D-Wave has a clearer path to making money than many quantum peers because companies can already use its systems and cloud service for real tasks. He also noted the company has enough cash to pursue deals and roll out new products faster.

However, Sankar warned that risks remain, including production hurdles and rising competition from other quantum technologies.

Overall, the analyst views D-Wave as an early commercial leader in quantum computing, already generating revenue with greater potential ahead as new systems roll out.

Is QBTS Stock a Good Stock to Invest In? 

Analysts remain highly optimistic about D-Wave's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, QBTS stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 12 unanimous Buys. The average D-Wave Quantum price target of $41.25 implies 110% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Standard Chartered Says Bitcoin (BTC) Will Bottom at $50,000 in 'Next Few Months'
data.description:
British bank Standard Chartered (STAN) has lowered its price target on Bitcoin (BTC) and said that the largest cryptocurrency should bottom at $50,000 within the "next few months."

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The bank, long one of the biggest crypto bulls, lowered its forecast for Bitcoin's price to $100,000 by year-end, down from $150,000 previously. "I think we are going to see more pain and a final capitulation period for digital asset prices in the next few months," wrote analyst Geoff Kendrick in a note to clients.

Last September, Kendrick had forecast that Bitcoin would reach $200,000 by the end of 2025 and be at $500,000 by 2028. Those expectations have been ratcheted down with BTC now trading at $66,300, having been effectively cut in half from an all-time high of $126,000 reached last October.

The Crypto Winter Will Thaw

Kendrick added in his latest note to clients that the average Bitcoin exchange-traded fund (ETF) holding is down 25%, and with the crypto market becoming more challenging, "holders are more likely to sell, rather than buy the dip, for now."

Still, Kendrick, who is head of digital assets research at Standard Chartered, expects the current crypto winter to thaw, likely this spring once there is a change of leadership at the U.S. Federal Reserve. "The macro backdrop is unlikely to provide support until we near Warsh taking over at the Fed," he wrote.

Is Bitcoin a Buy?

Most analysts don't offer ratings or price targets on Bitcoin. So instead, we'll look at the three-month performance of BTC. As one can see in the chart below, the price of Bitcoin has declined 30% in the last 12 weeks.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: How Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) Powers the Bullish Options Trade
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM), or more commonly known as TSMC, is no longer a hot contrarian trade within the tech ecosystem. What I mean is that you can't really harp on the artificial intelligence narrative as if it's a forward-looking novelty. People already know about its relevance to machine learning, and that enthusiasm is already priced into TSM stock. That by itself, however, doesn't necessarily mean that the security is no longer a bullish opportunity.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Again, what I'm about to say is already priced in, but it's worth recapping for broader context. Fundamentally, TSMC sits upstream in the value chain within the AI industry. While frontline semiconductor companies that produce the latest and greatest graphics processors tend to grab the lion's share of the spotlight, these entities must compete downstream within the finished goods ecosystem. That's likely going to cause sharp peaks and troughs, which attract a certain kind of speculator.

TSMC has the good fortune of being a foundry. As such, it's able to absorb the general sentiment of AI enthusiasm. For downstream players, their finished products must actually beat out their rivals for them to benefit. In other words, TSMC is involved in selling tickets to the game, not picking out the eventual winner of the matchup. Overall, this dynamic means that TSM stock is "boring," but it offers more reliability, thus attracting more conservative-leaning speculators. Even asset management firms are getting in on the action.

What's especially compelling is how institutional investors are signaling that TSM's bullish momentum is likely to continue well into the future.

Volatility Skew Does Much Heavy Lifting for TSM Stock

One of the most important first-order (observational) analytical tools is "volatility skew." This screener identifies implied volatility (IV) — or a stock's potential kinetic output — across the strike price spectrum of a given options chain. For the March 20 expiration date, the skew shows a slight hint of the smart money prioritizing upside convexity.
data.author: Joshua Enomoto

data.title: Options Markets Fires Warning Shot at Advanced Micro Devices Stock (AMD)
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) will likely be priced higher than where it currently stands if technological trends continue the way they have. Almost certainly, artificial intelligence is more than just a passing fad. With greater integration of automated solutions and processes, the company's graphics processors will likely be a hot commodity. As such, the long-term picture of AMD stock appears optimistic.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

However, the options market presents a much more agnostic view. For one thing, traders are primarily focused on short-term profits. In other words, these sophisticated market participants will go where the wind blows — and right now, the wind is exactly blowing favorably for AMD stock. Yes, it had a big day on Friday, gaining more than 8%. However, since the start of the year, the security is down roughly 3%.
data.author: Joshua Enomoto

data.title: Why Is Silver Down 10% Today, 2/12/26?
data.description:
Silver (XAGUSD) is plunging on Thursday amid a rout in the stock market as investors take off risk. The downturn in stocks has forced some investors to reduce exposure to precious metals in order to raise capital and meet margin requirements.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"For gold and silver, a great deal of trading remains sentiment and momentum driven," said Saxo Bank commodity strategist Ole Hansen. "On days like these they will struggle."

Silver Pulls Back ahead of Key Inflation Data

Profit-taking is also likely responsible for silver's disappointing performance today amid a 136% rally over the past year. MLIV Macro Strategist Michael Ball added that algo selling could be a driving factor behind the selloff as well.

Silver is set to receive a major catalyst when the Bureau of Labor Statistics releases January's Consumer Price Index (CPI), which tracks inflation, on Friday morning. A higher-than-expected reading could benefit silver, as it is viewed as a hedge against inflation and a store of value when purchasing power declines.
